title: melhania itampoloensis
intro: Melhania itampoloensis, also known as Malabar nut or Ilamathi, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, native to the tropical regions of Sri Lanka and India. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, and its leaves are traditionally used to make herbal preparations. The health benefits of Melhania itampoloensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by drying the leaves, roots, or flowers, and then infusing them in hot water to create teas or decoctions.
